figures.caption: Table 6: Estimations of the Lipschitz constants of ELMs to the heat equation in Example 5 with n = 3, for different numbers of collocation (training) points (N ) and numbers of hidden units (m), i.e., network width. We use L = ∥β∥∥W∥ as an over-approximation of the true Lipschitz constants. The test error is the maximum true error computed at 2N test points.
Figure 1: Neural network solutions with three hidden layers and 10 neurons each, compared with the fourth-order RungeKutta method.
Table 5: Verification of generalization error for Burgers equation: all errors are verified using ∂-CROWN [Eiras et al., 2024] with the generalization error calculated using Corollary 8.1.
